title: New unicorns, summer deals and sales records in the global tech market
summary: The tech sector sees nearly 90 new unicorns, Cyberpunk 2077 reaches 40 million copies, and Samsung foldable prices rise in Europe.

title: Massive hardware analysis: laptops, monitors, GPUs and peripherals for all budgets
summary: New laptops with RTX 5070, affordable OLED monitors, and detailed reviews of the RTX 5090 and ThinkPad X1 mark the current state of hardware.

title: Sony clarifies its stance on physical games, inactive accounts, and PS5 disc drive shortage
summary: The company confirms it will continue reprinting disc games after 2028, defends closing inactive accounts, and admits to purchase limits on the disc drive.

title: Hollywood confronts artificial intelligence: sues Midjourney and calls for banning Seedance
summary: The film industry demands AI tools reveal their training data while seeking to block unauthorized use of their works.

title: The Fable engine and classic game ports revolutionize browser and mobile gaming
summary: Half-Life 2 runs in the browser at over 100 FPS, Command & Conquer Generals comes to macOS and iOS, and a tool is launched to reduce token costs for Claude Code and Fable 5.

title: Health, wellness, and digital detox: the risk of cannabis and the power of silence and walks
summary: Studies confirm the link between cannabis and heart attacks, while science supports movement and meditation to improve creativity and mental health.

title: Cybersecurity, energy, and infrastructure: from polluting data centers to nuclear batteries for drones
summary: Meta suspends its data centers due to pollution, the FBI catches a young Estonian hacker linked to Scattered Spider, and DARPA plans nuclear batteries for military drones.

title: Sony made a PS1 controller hidden inside a DualShock, but canceled it before seeing the light
summary: The 'PlayStation Puga' project offered a full console integrated into a controller, but was discarded due to high cost and low margin for developers.

title: Alibaba internally bans use of Claude Code over alleged espionage and backdoor in China
summary: The Chinese tech company orders its employees to migrate to its own tool Qoder following the controversy generated by Anthropic's software.

title: Google sparks new debate with ad where AI writes the U.S. Declaration of Independence
summary: The commercial imagines an alternate past where the founding fathers use artificial intelligence to draft the historic founding document.

title: Mistral AI, the European rival to OpenAI, grows amid controversies over proprietary models and corporate privacy
summary: The French startup gains prominence as its CEO warns of the risk of companies exposing their internal processes by using paid artificial intelligence.

title: Chinese YMTC SSDs arrive in Lenovo laptops amid performance criticism
summary: The Chinese NAND memory company makes the leap to the international OEM market, although initial tests indicate performance below the industry average.

title: Intel prepares its Nova Lake-S desktop processors with new Xe3P graphics and 22 cores
summary: Intel's future CPUs seek to compete with AMD Ryzen X3D thanks to high caches and improved graphics support in the Linux kernel.

title: Valve's hardware legacy: Steam controller prototypes and fan-made consoles
summary: An early Steam Controller prototype appears in a second-hand store, while enthusiasts build their own homemade Steam Machine version.

title: Samsung raises DRAM prices and prepares new 990 PCIe 4.0 SSD for the retail market
summary: The Korean giant plans a 20% increase in its memories for the third quarter and launches a mysterious new solid-state drive model.

title: Gaming reinvents itself: magnetic keyboards, precision trackballs, and the unstoppable success of idle games
summary: Alternative peripherals and incremental games challenge established trends, marking a new direction in interactive entertainment.

title: Technology in Spain: Digi reported for blocking LaLiga, Orange launches WiFi 7, and automatic driving license gains followers
summary: The national tech news is marked by communications privacy, new fiber optics, and changing driving habits towards automatic.

title: From the Egyptian pyramids to Mars: the great milestones of science and exploration in the last week
summary: New data on Earth's magnetic field, the origin of the pyramids, the resistance of Martian bacteria, and jellyfish healing focus scientific attention.

title: The climate crisis tightens: Rhine drought, heat waves, and innovative solutions in the Mediterranean
summary: Water shortage threatens European logistics, urban tree planting becomes essential against heat waves, and renewable energy and circular economy gain ground.

title: The development world embraces artificial intelligence and new productivity tools
summary: From the migration of Shadcn/UI to Base UI to tips for reducing assumptions in code, the technical community never rests in its quest for efficiency and software quality.
